WMTS.description:
Loads a MapColonies raster layer into OpenLayers via the WMTS capabilities document. Shows how to authenticate with a token, parse capabilities, and build a tile source.

Use this as the starting point for any 2D map backed by MapColonies tiles — it covers the auth + capabilities handshake you would otherwise have to wire up yourself.

OverviewMap.description:
Adds an OpenLayers OverviewMap control — a minimap in the corner that mirrors the main view.

Gives users spatial context when zoomed in deeply, without you having to build a synced second map by hand.

Permalink.description:
Keeps the map center, zoom and rotation in sync with the URL hash, and restores them on reload.

Lets users share a deep-link to any view and bookmark map state — no backend persistence needed.

QueryService.description:
Queries the MapColonies vector WFS endpoint and renders matching features on top of the raster basemap.

Shows the request shape and response handling so you can filter and display server-side data without writing the WFS plumbing yourself.

InteractiveFeatureSelect.description:
Drag-box selection over vector features, with hit testing and styled selection state.

Drop-in pattern for bulk operations (export, edit, delete) on map features — saves you from reinventing the box-drag + selection bookkeeping.

InteractiveSensitiveBuildings.description:
Loads sensitive building features, highlights them on hover, and exposes attribute data on click.

Reference for domain-specific POI workflows where styling, hit detection and attribute panels need to work together.

Debug Layer.description:
Overlays the WMTS tile grid with tile coordinates and matrix IDs.

Use during integration to verify that your tile matrix set, origin and resolutions actually line up — turns 'why is my map blank' into a visible diagnosis.

DrawAndModify.description:
Draw and edit points, lines and polygons with geodesic geometry — distances and areas are computed on the ellipsoid, not the projected plane.

If you need measurement-correct sketching (AOIs, range rings, routes), this saves you from the classic 'planar distance looks wrong at high latitudes' bug.

PreloadTiles.description:
Configures the tile layer to preload neighbouring zoom levels in the background.

Result: pan and zoom feel snappier because the next tiles are already in cache. Useful when you need a smooth UX on slow networks without writing a custom tile cache.

DrawStreetLabels.description:
Renders street-name labels from a vector source with collision avoidance and rotation-aware placement.

Shortcut to readable labelling on top of raster basemaps — the placement/rotation logic is where naive implementations usually fall over.

ol-ext.description:
Integrates the ol-ext extension library on top of the MapColonies WMTS basemap, showcasing ready-made controls and interactions.

Use it as a survey of off-the-shelf widgets (legends, layer switchers, popups) so you don't end up reimplementing common UI from scratch.

WMTS.description:
Wires a MapColonies WMTS raster layer into Cesium as a WebMapTileServiceImageryProvider, using the capabilities document to pick the right tile matrix set.

Start here for any 3D globe view backed by MapColonies imagery — the capabilities + tilingScheme matching is the part most integrators get wrong.

GeoCoding.description:
Sends user-typed queries to the MapColonies geocoding service and flies the Cesium camera to matching results.

Drop-in 'search this place' UX for 3D viewers — covers request shape, result ranking and camera animation in one example.

Terrain.description:
Attaches a MapColonies DEM as a Cesium terrain provider so the globe has real elevation underneath the imagery.

Gives you photorealistic relief and correct picking heights without having to host or tile your own DEM.

3D Model.description:
Composes the full MapColonies 3D stack: WMTS imagery, DEM terrain and a photo-realistic 3D Tiles mesh, all aligned in one scene.

Reference for end-to-end 3D scene assembly — shows the loading order and provider config that makes imagery, terrain and meshes line up correctly.

LayersSplit.description:
Splits the Cesium view between two imagery layers with a draggable divider — left side shows layer A, right side shows layer B.

Ready-made before/after compare UX for change detection, version review or A/B imagery without writing the splitter math yourself.

ViewShed.description:
Computes a viewshed (visible / not visible) from an observer point over the 3D Tiles mesh and DEM, using a custom shader.

Line-of-sight analysis for planning, security and coverage use cases — the shader plus depth-buffer trick is the hard part this example does for you.

WMTS.description:
Minimal Leaflet map consuming the MapColonies WMTS service via a plain L.tileLayer.

Good entry point when you want a lightweight 2D map without OpenLayers — shows the URL template and token handling needed to hit the same backend.
